title: verbascum × nothum subsp. subphlomoides
intro: Verbascum × nothum subsp. subphlomoides, also known as Mullein or Velvet Plant, is a biennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia, belonging to the figwort family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in its leaves and flowers, which contain mucilage, saponins, and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, making it useful in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Verbascum × nothum subsp. subphlomoides include soothing irritated mucous membranes, reducing inflammation, and promoting relaxation. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, salves, and tinctures, making it a popular choice among herbalists and natural health practitioners.
